core_case.question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $72,000 on April 12?
core_case.market_title: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $72,000 on April 12?
core_case.contract_ambiguity_reason: live assignment baseline 0.76 conflicted with fetched Polymarket 72k line around 0.36
core_case.freshness_driver: the exact noon ET Binance 1-minute close and late-morning BTC path
decision_blockers: Assignment market baseline of 0.76 conflicted with live fetched Polymarket pricing near 0.36
Outcome is highly path-dependent in the final hours before noon ET
No synthesis-stage direct check of the actual resolving noon candle was possible yet

structured_handoff_primary.contract_ambiguity_reason: live assignment baseline 0.76 conflicted with fetched Polymarket 72k line around 0.36
independently_verified_points: Polymarket rules require Binance BTC/USDT exact 12:00 ET 1-minute Close strictly above 72,000
Fetched Polymarket page showed the 72,000 line around 36% at synthesis time
Fresh Binance BTCUSDT spot during synthesis was about 71,728, still below 72,000
Fresh Binance 24h range included 72,000 and recent 1m closes remained below threshold
structured_handoff_primary.verification_gap_summary: The main remaining gap is final-hour path information into the decisive noon ET candle.
structured_handoff_primary.best_countercase_summary: BTC only needed a routine sub-1% move and 72,000 was already within the recent 24h range, so a normal morning rally could still flip Yes.
structured_handoff_primary.main_reason_for_disagreement: Most disagreement came from stale or mismatched market baseline inputs rather than substantive mechanism disagreement.
structured_handoff_primary.resolution_mechanics_summary: Yes requires the final Close of Binance BTC/USDT’s exact 12:00 ET 1-minute candle on April 12 to be strictly above 72,000.
structured_handoff_primary.freshness_driver: the exact noon ET Binance 1-minute close and late-morning BTC path
decision_blockers: Assignment market baseline of 0.76 conflicted with live fetched Polymarket pricing near 0.36
Outcome is highly path-dependent in the final hours before noon ET
No synthesis-stage direct check of the actual resolving noon candle was possible yet

verification_enforcement.rationale: Decision-Maker should use the deterministic selected-input bundle as its verification boundary; additional agent-side tool use is disallowed in this mode.

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: Market pricing is broadly efficient but slightly optimistic on Yes because Binance BTC/USDT is still below 72000 and the contract requires the exact noon ET 1-minute close to finish above that threshold.
main_logical_chain: Start from market-implied probability around 37% Yes.
Verify the governing contract mechanics on Polymarket: Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute close must be strictly above 72000.
Check current Binance pricing context: spot and recent minute closes are below 72000.
Because the threshold is close, a meaningful Yes probability is justified, but the exact-minute close requirement makes it somewhat harder than a generic threshold-touch framing.
strongest_supports: Binance spot was about 71394, below the 72000 threshold.
strongest_disconfirmers: BTC only needs a modest roughly 0.8% move to clear 72000 with many hours remaining.
Crypto can move that distance intraday without a major headline catalyst.
unresolved_ambiguities: How much intraday volatility will emerge before the noon ET candle.
Whether any unobserved catalyst appears before settlement.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: I would move up if Binance BTC/USDT reclaims and sustains 72000+ into the morning ET window or if a clear upside catalyst emerges before settlement.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: High: this is a date-specific and minute-specific contract with about 14 hours remaining from the reviewed snapshot to settlement.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: High on contract mechanics and settlement source because Polymarket rules and Binance data are direct; independence is medium because both reflect the same BTC market state.

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: The 72k line is a plausible but still sub-even-odds threshold, and without a concrete upside catalyst before noon ET the exact Binance 1-minute close is more likely to finish below than above 72,000.
main_logical_chain: Interpret the exact contract mechanics and source of truth.
Use the visible 72k ladder price as the market-implied baseline.
strongest_supports: Rules require the exact Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute final close to be strictly above 72,000.
strongest_disconfirmers: Only one minute close above 72,000 is needed, so ordinary crypto volatility can still flip the outcome near the threshold.
unresolved_ambiguities: Assignment metadata showed 0.76 while the visible 72k line on the governing page showed about 0.34.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: A direct upside catalyst or sustained Binance trading above 72k before the final hour would raise the estimate materially.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: This is primarily a catalyst-window and exact-settlement-timing market, not a broad long-run Bitcoin thesis market.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: Strong on contract wording and visible market state from Polymarket, weaker on independent live-price context because secondary extraction was poor.
